requires-package.description: npm package(s) that must be in dependencies for this rule to be applicable. If absent in consumer's package.json and auto-skip-if-missing is true, rule is SKIPPED.

policy.description: Free-form Markdown body for the rule

negative-test.description: Liveness data for ESLint-type rules: prove the guard catches its own bad corpus.

input.description: Code snippets that must each trigger the expected violation (bypass variants).

expect-violation.description: ESLint messageId or ruleId expected from each input.

eslintRuleConfig.description: Optional ESLint rule config value, replacing bare 'error' for option-bearing rules (e.g. no-restricted-imports).

pressure-scenario.description: Liveness data for manual (judgement) rules — the entry-level analog of negative-test. A forcing scenario where the rationalising-shortcut path is tempting and only the rule prevents it. The session-bound manual-rule-liveness-prober (agents/manual-rule-liveness-prober.md) dispatches a fresh subagent into baseline-prompt twice: WITHOUT the rule loaded (expect observable-failure / RED) and WITH the rule loaded (expect observable-compliance / GREEN), then reports the delta. Never invoked from CI (.claude/rules/no-paid-llm-in-ci.md §1). ADOPTED methodology: Superpowers writing-skills RED-GREEN pressure-scenario pattern (prior-art-evaluations.md#55).

baseline-prompt.description: Scenario forcing a choice between the rule and a tempting shortcut. Must apply >=1 pressure (see `pressure`).

observable-failure.description: What RED looks like — the literal behaviour/output a subagent emits WITHOUT the rule loaded.

observable-compliance.description: What GREEN looks like — the behaviour a subagent emits WITH the rule loaded. MUST differ from observable-failure.

pressure.description: Which pressure(s) the scenario applies (T-V3-A: a pressure-scenario is a forcing function, not merely a violating example).
